headline: Washington's balanced attack overwhelms Oklahoma in season opener
body: Washington made the most of a decisive yardage advantage to dispatch Oklahoma 25-18 in Week 1, establishing early control through a ground game that set the tempo for the entire afternoon. The Huskies' 367 total yards and balanced offensive attack proved too much for an Oklahoma defense that spent much of the game chasing plays rather than dictating them. Demond Williams Jr. delivered when it mattered, completing 19 of 29 passes for 265 yards and two touchdowns while managing the game with the kind of poise that suggests Washington's offensive identity will lean heavily on his decision-making.

The margin of victory hinged on Washington's ability to convert opportunities into points while Oklahoma's offensive inconsistency became increasingly costly as the game wore on. Glass managed two touchdown passes for the Sooners but was limited to just 144 yards, a figure that underscores how little room for error existed in an offense that couldn't establish rhythm on the ground. Washington's Bonner Jr. rushed for 91 yards and a touchdown on eight carries, providing the kind of ground support that kept Oklahoma's pass rush from dictating terms. The Huskies also won the turnover battle 2-1, including a crucial fumble recovery that eliminated a drive Oklahoma desperately needed.

Washington's defensive pressure, anchored by Karnley's four tackles and two sacks, never allowed Oklahoma to settle into a comfortable pattern. The Sooners' 215 total yards represented the kind of output that rarely results in wins at this level, and the early turnover on the Oklahoma side—coming at a moment when the game was still undecided—proved to be the inflection point. This was a convincing statement of intent from Washington, which controlled both lines of scrimmage and forced Oklahoma to play a reactive brand of football from the opening drive.
turningPoint: Oklahoma's second fumble in the second half came when Washington's defense had already seized momentum, converting the turnover into a score that effectively put the game beyond reach.

unsungHero.name: Bonner Jr.
unsungHero.why: His 91-yard rushing performance and touchdown were the foundation that allowed Williams Jr. to operate in favorable down-and-distance situations, yet the quarterback earned Player of the Game recognition while the run game's effectiveness went understated.
